mnemonics: DIG FACTS: Business dimensions are Descriptive, Independent, Grouping, Frequently Analyzed, Consistent, Textual, and Slowly Changing.
keyPoints: Dimensions provide context for facts (e.g., who, what, where, when)
They are descriptive attributes used for filtering, grouping, and labeling data
They are typically textual and slowly changing over time
commonMistakes: Confusing dimensions with facts (dimensions are descriptive, facts are measurable)
Creating too many or overly complex dimensions that complicate the model
Improperly handling slowly changing dimensions, leading to historical inaccuracies
